message:
fix(runtime): drive region state machine after task/obligation completion

The region lifecycle was not being driven automatically when tasks completed
or obligations resolved. This fix adds `advance_region_state()` calls at the
critical points where region prerequisites change:

Key changes:
- Call `advance_region_state()` after obligation resolution (commit, abort, timeout)
- Call `advance_region_state()` when a task exits in `mark_task_exited()`
- Add new `advance_region_state()` method that drives the state machine:
  - Closing/Draining → Finalizing when no children remain
  - Finalizing → Closed when finalizers complete and prerequisites met
  - Recursively advances parent regions when a child closes

- Refactor `run_sync_finalizers()` to return the first async barrier instead
  of collecting all async finalizers. This enables proper sequencing: sync
  finalizers run immediately, then the caller schedules the async barrier,
  and calls again when that completes.

This ensures regions progress through their lifecycle automatically rather
than requiring external polling, fixing potential hangs where `cx.region`
would never return because the region state machine wasn't being driven.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
